specification.topic-statement: The OpenAI message serializer transforms canonical conversation messages into the format required by the Chat Completions API or the Responses API, producing distinct output structures for each API endpoint.

scope.in-scope: Role mapping from canonical roles to OpenAI role strings, the Chat Completions message object format for tool results, tool calls, and plain text messages, the Responses API item format for each content block type, and the text block concatenation rule shared by both APIs.
scope.boundary: The Chat Completions request builder calls the Chat message serializer for each canonical message and places the result in the messages array. The request-level assembly is covered by the OpenAI chat request specification.

The Responses API request builder calls the Responses message serializer for each canonical message and accumulates items into the input array. The request-level assembly is covered by the OpenAI responses request specification.
Serialization of tool definitions (name, description, parameter schema) is part of request building, not message serialization, and is covered by the respective request builder specifications.

field.name: content blocks
field.note: zero or more blocks of type text, tool_call, or tool_result

field.name: id
field.note: unique identifier for this tool call

field.name: name
field.note: name of the function being called

field.name: arguments
field.note: JSON-encoded argument object as a string

field.name: tool_call_id
field.note: identifier of the tool call this result responds to

field.name: content
field.note: the result text returned by the tool

behavior.role-mapping:
Canonical roles map to OpenAI role strings as follows:
- user role: "user" - assistant role: "assistant" - tool role: "tool"
The role string appears in the "role" field of every Chat Completions message object, and in the "role" field of text items in the Responses API input array. Tool call and tool result items in the Responses API format do not carry a "role" field.
An unrecognized canonical role value causes an immediate abnormal termination. There is no fallback or default mapping for unknown roles.
behavior.chat-completions-format:
A Chat Completions message object is constructed as a single JSON object. The content of that object depends on the message role and the types of content blocks present:
When the message role is tool, the object contains four fields: "role" set to "tool", "tool_call_id" taken from the first content block's tool result identifier, and "content" taken from that block's result text. The role is set before the tool-specific fields.
Only the first content block is examined for a tool result message. If the block type is not a tool result, neither "tool_call_id" nor "content" is added to the message object.
When the message contains at least one tool call content block (regardless of role), the object contains "role" set to "assistant", "content" set to null, and a "tool_calls" array. The "tool_calls" array contains one entry per tool call content block in the message, in their original order. Non-tool-call blocks are skipped. Each entry in the array contains: "id" with the tool call identifier, "type" set to "function", and a nested "function" object containing "name" and "arguments" (the arguments JSON string passed through verbatim as a string, not parsed).
Tool call messages set "content" explicitly to null rather than omitting the field. This is a required structure for the Chat Completions API.
For all other messages, the object contains "role" and "content". The content is formed by concatenating all text blocks in the message with "\n\n" as the separator between consecutive blocks. If there are no text blocks, "content" is set to an empty string.
The role is inspected first. A message with role tool is treated as a tool result message unconditionally. Only messages with other roles are examined for tool call blocks.
behavior.responses-api-format:
The Responses API serializer expands a single canonical message into zero or more input array items. Each content block in the message is translated individually into a separate item. Blocks are processed in their original order. Content block types not recognized by the serializer are silently skipped and produce no output item.
A text block produces an object with two fields: "role" set to the message's mapped role string, and "content" set to the text block's text value. Each text block becomes its own item; blocks are not concatenated across the array form.
In the Responses API array format, each text block becomes a separate item with its own "role" and "content". The "\n\n" concatenation rule applies only when a single user message with text-only content triggers the string input path; it does not apply to array-format serialization.
A tool call block produces an object with four fields: "type" set to "function_call", "call_id" set to the tool call identifier, "name" set to the tool function name, and "arguments" set to the arguments JSON string verbatim. No "role" field is present.
A tool result block produces an object with three fields: "type" set to "function_call_output", "call_id" set to the tool call identifier this result responds to, and "output" set to the result content text. No "role" field is present.
behavior.text-concatenation-rule: Wherever the Chat Completions format assembles a text content string from a message, and wherever the Responses API string input path assembles its single string value, text blocks are joined using "\n\n" as the separator. The separator is inserted only between consecutive text blocks; it is not prepended before the first block or appended after the last. Non-text blocks (tool calls, tool results) are excluded from the concatenation entirely, neither contributing text nor affecting separator placement.
